title: The Breath Before Sleep
style: kawaii-zen, soft pastels, dorm room nighttime
language_notes: English with Japanese aesthetic terms. Ma (間) -- the meaningful pause, the space between. Mono no aware (ものの哀れ) -- the bittersweet awareness of impermanence.

panels.visual_prompt: Evening. Nana stands in the middle of her dorm room holding her phone in both hands. She looks at it, then looks at her desk across the room. The desk is cluttered with textbooks and a small cactus. Warm lamplight. The roommate is out. Nana's face shows determination mixed with dread.
panels.narration: Nine PM. Nana holds her phone and looks at the far side of the room. Six steps away. She counts them.
panels.body_anchor: phone gripped in both hands, feet rooted, counting distance

panels.visual_prompt: Close-up sequence: three small panels in a row. First: Nana's hand placing the phone face-down on the desk. Second: her fingers letting go, pulling away. Third: the phone sits alone on the desk, small and dark, next to the cactus. The letting-go is rendered as a deliberate, physical act.
panels.narration: She walks the six steps. She sets the phone on the desk. She lifts her fingers away, one at a time, like peeling tape.
panels.body_anchor: fingers peeling away from phone one by one

panels.visual_prompt: Wide shot. Nana climbs into bed. The room is dimmer now -- only the desk lamp remains on. She pulls the blanket to her chin. Her eyes dart toward the desk where the phone sits. The six steps between bed and desk feel enormous in the composition, stretched by the wide angle.
panels.narration: She gets into bed. The phone sits six steps away. It might as well be six miles. She turns off the lamp.
panels.body_anchor: blanket pulled to chin, eyes darting toward desk

panels.visual_prompt: Dark room. Only moonlight now. Nana lies on her back, eyes open, staring at the ceiling. The ceiling is rendered as a vast, empty expanse of soft gray. Thought shapes begin to form at the edges of the panel -- dark watercolor forms creeping in from the margins. An exam. A phone call owed. A deadline.
panels.narration: The dark fills the room. The thoughts arrive on schedule. The exam. Her mother's voice on the missed call. The group chat she muted.
panels.body_anchor: eyes open wide, chest tightening, fingers gripping blanket edge

panels.visual_prompt: Close-up of Nana's hands gripping the blanket. Her knuckles are white. The watercolor thought-shapes press closer from the panel edges. She is bracing against them, holding tight.
panels.narration: Her hands grip the blanket. She braces. The old reflex says: reach for the phone. Drown the thoughts in scrolling. She grips tighter instead.
panels.body_anchor: knuckles white on blanket, whole body bracing

panels.visual_prompt: Medium shot. Nana on her back, still gripping. But now something shifts in the composition. The watercolor thought-shapes stop advancing. They hover at the panel edges, present but no longer closing in. Nana's grip on the blanket loosens one degree. Her jaw unclenches one fraction.
panels.narration: The thoughts do not leave. But she stops pushing against them. She lets them sit at the edges of the room like furniture she did not choose but cannot move.
panels.body_anchor: grip loosening one degree, jaw releasing one fraction

panels.visual_prompt: Extreme close-up of Nana's chest and collarbone area, seen from above. Her ribcage rises. The blanket lifts. This is just a breath -- rendered large and slow. The fabric of the blanket has a gentle, wave-like quality to its folds. Moonlight catches the rise.
panels.narration: She notices her chest rise. Just that. The breath comes in. She does not shape it or slow it or count it. She just watches it happen.
panels.body_anchor: chest rising, ribcage expanding, watching without changing

panels.visual_prompt: Same framing. The chest falls. The blanket settles. The exhale. The panel is slightly smaller than the inhale panel, creating a visual rhythm. The moonlight dims a fraction as a cloud passes. Ma -- the pause between breaths is rendered as white space between this panel and the next.
panels.narration: The breath goes out. A pause follows. Ma. The space between. She rests inside it.
panels.body_anchor: chest falling, exhale completing, resting in the gap

panels.visual_prompt: Wide shot of the room. The watercolor thought-shapes at the panel edges have softened. They are still present but their colors have faded from dark indigo to soft lavender. Nana lies still, hands now resting open-palmed on the blanket instead of gripping. The moonlight has found the floor and makes a silver rectangle.
panels.narration: The thoughts remain. The exam has not vanished. Her mother still waits for a call. But the thoughts have lost their teeth. They sit in the room like old coats hanging on hooks.
panels.body_anchor: hands open-palmed, not gripping, body sinking into mattress

panels.visual_prompt: Close-up of Nana's face. Her eyes are half-closed now, not squeezed shut but drifting. Her jaw is loose -- visibly different from every previous panel. The tension line that ran from ear to chin has softened. Her lips are slightly parted. Moonlight touches her cheekbone.
panels.narration: Her jaw releases. The muscle she has held tight since midterms goes slack, and the absence of pain registers like a sound she can finally hear because the noise stopped.
panels.body_anchor: jaw slack, masseter released, lips parted, face softening

panels.visual_prompt: Medium shot. Nana on her side now, curled gently, not tightly. Her breathing is visible in the soft rise and fall of the blanket. The roommate's empty bed is in the background. The phone on the desk is a tiny dark shape, forgotten. The room is silver and indigo and quiet.
panels.narration: She is not asleep. She knows she is not asleep. But she is here. Her body occupies the bed fully, not perched at its edge, not ready to spring for the phone. Present. Mono no aware -- the tender ache of being inside a passing moment.
panels.body_anchor: body curled gently, breathing slow, fully occupying the bed

panels.visual_prompt: Final panel. The room from outside the window. We see the dorm window from the courtyard -- a small dark rectangle with the faintest shape of Nana resting inside. The window is one of many in the building, but a soft glow of moonlight picks it out. Cherry blossom branches frame the foreground, buds not yet open. Stars visible. The building breathes.
panels.narration: From outside, the window is dark. One room among dozens. Inside, Nana breathes. The night holds the building and the building holds the room and the room holds a girl learning to hold nothing at all.
panels.body_anchor: breath slow, body at rest, held by the room
